ingredients.item: 2 teaspoons kosher salt, or more to taste
ingredients.original: 2 teaspoons kosher salt, or more to taste

ingredients.item: 3 cups (550 g) long-grain white basmati rice, rinsed until the water runs clear and drained completely
ingredients.original: 3 cups (550 g) long-grain white basmati rice, rinsed until the water runs clear and drained completely

ingredients.item: 1 In a large pot, heat the oil for 1 minute over medium heat. Add the onion and cook, stirring often, until softened and translucent, 5 to 6 minutes.
ingredients.original: 1 In a large pot, heat the oil for 1 minute over medium heat. Add the onion and cook, stirring often, until softened and translucent, 5 to 6 minutes.

ingredients.item: 2 Add the peppercorns, cloves, cardamom, and cinnamon sticks to the pot and cook, stirring continuously, for a few more minutes until the spices are toasted and fragrant. Add the salt and turmeric and stir to combine with the onion and spices.
ingredients.original: 2 Add the peppercorns, cloves, cardamom, and cinnamon sticks to the pot and cook, stirring continuously, for a few more minutes until the spices are toasted and fragrant. Add the salt and turmeric and stir to combine with the onion and spices.

ingredients.item: 3 Add the rice and stir it gently to combine with the onion and spices. Add the broth and bring to a rolling boil over medium heat, then cover the pot with a tight-fitting lid, reduce the heat to low, and let cook for 20 minutes, undisturbed.
ingredients.original: 3 Add the rice and stir it gently to combine with the onion and spices. Add the broth and bring to a rolling boil over medium heat, then cover the pot with a tight-fitting lid, reduce the heat to low, and let cook for 20 minutes, undisturbed.

ingredients.item: 4 Turn off the heat, remove the lid, and gently fluff the rice with a fork or wooden spoon. Cover again and let rest for 10 minutes. Before serving, pick out and discard the peppercorns, cloves, cardamom pods and cinnamon sticks (see Note).
ingredients.original: 4 Turn off the heat, remove the lid, and gently fluff the rice with a fork or wooden spoon. Cover again and let rest for 10 minutes. Before serving, pick out and discard the peppercorns, cloves, cardamom pods and cinnamon sticks (see Note).
